evaluation.summary: The primorial-adjusted Benford null model is being mathematically derived by restricting Benford's Law to only digits coprime to the primorial base, yielding properly normalized probability distributions (e.g., for base 30: 8 valid coprime digits with d=1 dominating at 0.591), which provides a theoretically motivated alternative to standard Benford's Law for primes in primorial bases.
next_directions: Complete the derivation for Base-210 (p_4# = 210) and compute the full coprime-digit probability distribution to compare against empirically observed prime leading digit frequencies

Perform statistical hypothesis testing (chi-squared or KL divergence) between the primorial-adjusted Benford model and actual prime digit distributions up to 10^8 to assess goodness-of-fit versus naive Benford's Law

Investigate whether the residual deviation from the primorial-adjusted model follows a predictable pattern tied to the prime counting function or Chebyshev-type biases, potentially revealing deeper structure
